title: Watch SpaceX launch advanced GPS satellite for US Space Force early on April 20
summary: SpaceX is set to launch an advanced GPS satellite for the U.S. Space Force on April 20, with live coverage available. This mission marks another collaboration between SpaceX and the U.S. military.

title: Giant BlueBird 7 mobile phone satellite will be deorbited after faulty Blue Origin launch
summary: Blue Origin successfully recovered its first reused orbital-class rocket but failed to place the Giant BlueBird 7 mobile phone satellite in the correct orbit, leading to its deorbiting.

title: 'Sunshine' was 2007's answer to 'Project Hail Mary', and it told a much darker tale of solar catastrophe
summary: The article compares 'Sunshine' (2007) to 'Project Hail Mary', highlighting a darker narrative about solar catastrophe. It humorously notes the irony of needing sun cream in space.

title: Telescope livestream: Watch the moon pass in front of the Pleiades on April 19
summary: A telescope livestream will allow viewers to watch the moon pass in front of the Pleiades star cluster on April 19. This celestial event can be observed from home.

title: Synthetic universe allows you to 'see and hear' galaxies evolving from the dawn of time (video)
summary: Scientists created a synthetic universe simulation that accurately depicts the evolution of galaxies from the early universe. The simulation is so realistic that it has fooled some astronomers.

title: Pragmata's tale of AI slop, humanity, & lunar conquest makes it the timeliest sci-fi game of the year
summary: Capcom's new sci-fi game 'Pragmata' explores themes of AI, humanity, and lunar conquest with timely storytelling. The game is praised for its refreshing optimism and nostalgic gameplay.

title: Artemis 2 and Tiangong space station astronauts set record for farthest distance between humans
summary: On April 6, the Artemis 2 astronauts and Tiangong space station crew set a record for the farthest distance between humans in space. This marked a historic moment in international space cooperation.
company: NASA and China Manned Space Agency

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 206 — I Want to Believe
summary: This Week In Space podcast Episode 206 discusses the implications of discovering alien life, featuring guest Dr. Brianne Suldovsky from Portland State University.

title: CSDA Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Satellogic NewSat Data
summary: The CSDA Quality Assessment Report has evaluated Satellogic's NewSat data, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research and applications. This adds to the growing documentation on the value of commercial satellite data.

title: Webinar 4/29: NASA CSDA Program Vendor Focus- MDA Space
summary: NASA's CSDA program will host a webinar on April 29 featuring vendor MDA Space. The event aims to provide insights into the program.

title: NASA Artemis II Human Research Data Methodology Challenge
summary: NASA's Human Research Program (HRP) is developing methods to protect astronaut health for long-term missions on the Moon and Mars. The Artemis II mission was the first to utilize this research in preparation for deep space exploration.

title: NASA, OPM Announce New NASA Force Website, Open Job Applications
summary: NASA and the U.S. Office of Personnel Management launched the NASA Force website to recruit engineers and technologists for mission-critical roles in air and space programs. The initiative aims to fill high-impact technical positions supporting NASA's exploration, research, and advanced technology priorities.

title: Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Tomorrow.io Precipitation Radar Data
summary: A quality assessment report has evaluated the precipitation radar data from Tomorrow.io, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research. The report underscores the growing role of commercial data in scientific applications.

title: Volunteers Discover Rare Space Weather Events Using Their Ears
summary: Volunteers have discovered rare space weather events by analyzing data converted into sound, helping scientists understand the behavior of waves in Earth's magnetic field. NASA's Heliophysics Audified: Resonances in Plasmas (HARP) project transforms magnetic field data into audible frequencies for analysis.

title: NASA’s SPHEREx Observatory Maps Interstellar Ice in Milky Way
summary: NASA's SPHEREx Observatory has mapped interstellar ice in the Milky Way, detecting water ice and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons in the Cygnus X star-forming region. This discovery provides insights into the chemical composition of active star birth regions.

title: NASA Invites Media to Latvia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
summary: Latvia is set to sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington, hosted by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman. The event will include Latvia’s minister for education and science, Dace Melbārde, and other officials.

title: At the Edge of Light
summary: A photograph taken on April 6, 2026, captures the Moon's far side along the terminator, revealing long shadows and the Orientale Basin under low-angle sunlight.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after a failed launch on Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This incident follows Blue Origin's recent milestone of landing a previously flown first-stage booster.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an astronaut flight suit and a large docking adapter, combining fashion with practicality for future space missions. The company is developing commercial space station infrastructure.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fire tests as part of the Artemis program. This marks a significant milestone for the upcoming lunar missions.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew conducted a spacewalk and managed vehicle departures aboard the International Space Station in March. This marked another active month for the crew's operations.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn is preparing for its third launch with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled worldwide this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows the recent completion of Northrop Grumman's Cygnus S.S. William McCool CRS NG-23 mission.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. The mission marked a significant milestone for NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has debuted new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry development, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, held in Paris on June 8-9, 2026. The event includes an online preparatory forum in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The exact location for this achievement was not specified in the provided text.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has generated 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved precision or efficiency in directional control. It highlights a specific innovation or development in this area.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team used ESA's parabolic flight campaign to test graphene aerogels under microgravity conditions, demonstrating that lasers can propel the ultralight material with significant acceleration. The study, led by Université Libre de Bruxelles and Khalifa University, suggests potential applications for steering solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated this potential.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space industry applications. The chip's versatility could enhance satellite and spacecraft systems.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Thruster Market with Gauss
summary: Rocket Lab is entering the thruster market with its new Gauss product line. The company's CFO emphasized the importance of vertical integration for scaling production in the space industry.

title: Axiom Space’s Suit Set to Fly in 2027
summary: Axiom Space is preparing its first qualification spacesuit for a 2027 flight, with production ongoing despite external developments. Michael López-Alegría, the company's chief astronaut, confirmed the suit's progress in an interview with Payload.

title: Voyager Technologies Wins Its First Private ISS Mission
summary: Voyager Technologies has secured its first private mission to the International Space Station (ISS) in 2026, breaking Axiom Space's monopoly on NASA's Private Astronaut Missions (PAMs). This marks a shift in the commercial spaceflight landscape with new competitors emerging.

title: Seraphim Space Aims To Shape Strategy With New Advisory Council
summary: Seraphim Space has formed a new advisory council to address key challenges in the space industry, including sustainability, regulations, national security, and international collaboration.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: ORBX ETF launches to track companies earning over half their revenue from space tech, including transportation, software, and satellite services. This follows a trend of increasing space tech IPOs.

title: Kepler Leads Team to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test the agency's 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The initiative aims to demonstrate advanced space-based communication technologies.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget challenges, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He highlights the passion and scientific potential of the space industry but questions the financial planning behind major projects.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: CLD companies challenge NASA's claims, while NASA invites them to disprove the statements. The dispute was discussed at the Space Symposium.

title: Slingshot Introduces AI-Powered Portal Platform
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched an AI-powered portal platform to help operators detect anomalies and make decisions efficiently. The platform consolidates multiple functions into a single interface.

title: ICON Prime Launches, Led By Former TX Rep
summary: ICON Prime has been launched as a new business unit by ICON, a robotic construction company, to collaborate with the Pentagon and NASA. The initiative is led by a former Texas representative.

title: IFC News Roundup from AIX
summary: The AIX Aircraft Interiors Expo highlighted satellite companies advocating for in-flight connectivity (IFC) solutions to airlines. Via Satellite reported on key announcements related to this sector.

title: Airlines See IFC as Way to Build Relationships With Customers
summary: Airlines are leveraging in-flight connectivity (IFC) as a key strategy to enhance customer relationships. This trend is highlighted in a report from Hamburg, Germany.

title: NorthStar Earth & Space to Go Public in SPAC Deal at $300M Valuation
summary: NorthStar Earth & Space plans to go public via a SPAC deal valued at $300 million. The Canadian space domain awareness company integrates ground-based systems for its operations.

title: ESA Taps Kepler to Lead Next Stage of HydRON Optical Program
summary: The European Space Agency has selected Kepler Communications as the prime contractor for HydRON Element 3, the next phase of a sovereign optical communications constellation. This marks a significant step in advancing optical communication technology in space.

title: Space Force, NATO Commanders Share Threat Perspectives
summary: U.S. Space Force is identifying new space capabilities and managing threats from adversaries while creating missions to defend assets. NATO commanders also shared their threat perspectives in a discussion.

title: Air Force Awarded Development Contract for Space-Based AMTI; Meink Says Risk Low
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force awarded a contract to multiple vendors for the development of space-based air moving target indication (AMTI). The initiative aims to enhance tracking capabilities with low risk, according to Meink.

title: Boeing and Millennium Space Systems Add Mid-Class Resolute Satellite Bus
summary: Boeing and its subsidiary Millennium Space Systems have introduced a new mid-class satellite platform called Resolute, offering enhanced capabilities compared to traditional small satellites. The Resolute satellite bus is designed to bridge the gap between small and larger satellite platforms.

title: Slingshot Aerospace Releases AI-Powered Analytics Platform for Space Operations
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched Portal, an AI-powered analytics platform for satellite data monitoring and analysis. The software integrates Slingshot’s data flows with external sources to enhance space operations.

title: Industry Builds the Case Around Architecture Options for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Reductions in satellite launch prices and growing demand for alternatives to terrestrial data centers are making orbital space data centers more appealing. The industry is exploring architecture options to support this emerging trend.

title: Transport Layer of PWSA Will Eventually Transition From SDA to New Acquisition Executive, Sandhoo Says
summary: The Space Force plans to transfer the communications layer of the Space Development Agency's Proliferated Warfighter Space Architecture (PWSA) to a new acquisition executive. This transition is part of the Space Force's updated acquisition structure.
